method: Unsurfaced determined by normalized title or exact URL match against site/src/content MDX frontmatter.

unsurfaced_entries.name: Learning Engineering Process Description & Diagram (ICICLE)
unsurfaced_entries.description: Official ICICLE description of the Learning Engineering process, including the iterative cycle diagram showing problem framing, design, measurement, and improvement steps.

unsurfaced_entries.name: Learning Engineering Case Guide 1.0 (ICICLE)
unsurfaced_entries.description: Google Slides deck walking through real LE case examples. Shows how the LE process is applied in practice: problem formulation, data collection, evidence-based iteration.

unsurfaced_entries.name: Five Whys Root-Cause Analysis Template (ICICLE)
unsurfaced_entries.description: Google Doc template for applying the Five Whys technique to identify root causes of learning performance gaps. Designed for the LE needs-analysis phase.

unsurfaced_entries.name: Fishbone / Cause-Effect Analysis Template (ICICLE)
unsurfaced_entries.description: Google Doc template for Ishikawa (fishbone) cause-and-effect diagrams in learning contexts. Used during LE problem framing and needs analysis to map contributing factors to learning gaps.

unsurfaced_entries.name: Performance Task Analysis Template (ICICLE)
unsurfaced_entries.description: Google Doc template for structured task decomposition in learning engineering. Guides practitioners through breaking complex performance into observable subtasks to inform instructional design.

unsurfaced_entries.name: Learning Engineering Evidence Decision Tracker (ICICLE)
unsurfaced_entries.description: Google Drive folder with templates for tracking design decisions alongside the evidence that motivated them throughout the LE process. Supports reproducible, evidence-based practice.

unsurfaced_entries.name: Learning Engineering Implementation Checklist (ICICLE)
unsurfaced_entries.description: Google Doc checklist for practitioners implementing a learning engineering project. Covers project phases from needs analysis through evaluation and dissemination.

unsurfaced_entries.name: Examples of Roles & Expertise on an LE Team (ICICLE)
unsurfaced_entries.description: Google Doc describing the multidisciplinary roles typically needed on a learning engineering team: learning scientists, data analysts, instructional designers, engineers, subject matter experts.

unsurfaced_entries.name: Learning Design Principles Toolkit (McEldoon, ICICLE)
unsurfaced_entries.description: Google Drive toolkit by Katherine McEldoon connecting cognitive science principles to practical learning design decisions. Bridges learning science theory and LE design practice.

unsurfaced_entries.name: Bror Saxberg — MIT Festival of Learning Keynote 2023 (YouTube)
unsurfaced_entries.description: Keynote address by Bror Saxberg at the 2023 MIT Festival of Learning. Covers the vision of learning engineering, the gap between learning science and practice, and the role of data-driven design.
unsurfaced_entries.notes: Harvested from sagroups.ieee.org/icicle/resources/ Dec 2025. URL is approximate — verify against ICICLE page.

unsurfaced_entries.name: QIP Learning Engineering Video Series (YouTube playlist)
unsurfaced_entries.description: YouTube playlist produced by Quality Information Partners featuring practitioners and researchers discussing learning engineering principles, case studies, and field development.

unsurfaced_entries.name: Invitation to Learning Engineering Webinar Series (ICICLE, YouTube)
unsurfaced_entries.description: YouTube series of ICICLE-hosted webinars introducing the learning engineering field to new practitioners, covering foundational concepts, tools, and community resources.

unsurfaced_entries.name: Silver Lining for Learning — Art & Science of Learning Engineering (podcast, 2023)
unsurfaced_entries.description: Episode of the Silver Lining for Learning podcast exploring learning engineering as both an art and a science — balancing rigorous measurement with creative instructional design.

unsurfaced_entries.name: Silver Lining for Learning — Human-Centered Methods in LE (podcast, 2023)
unsurfaced_entries.description: Episode of the Silver Lining for Learning podcast focused on human-centered design methods applied within the learning engineering process.

unsurfaced_entries.name: Baker, Boser & Snow (2022) — Learning Engineering: A View on Where the Field Is At
unsurfaced_entries.description: Open-access article surveying the state of the learning engineering field as of 2022. Reviews definitions, practice norms, evidence standards, and key open problems.
unsurfaced_entries.notes: Harvested from sagroups.ieee.org/icicle/resources/ Dec 2025. APA Open access.

unsurfaced_entries.name: Craig et al. (2023) — Learning Engineering Perspectives for Educational Systems
unsurfaced_entries.description: Multi-author article framing how learning engineering perspectives can transform educational systems at scale. Addresses policy, infrastructure, and community dimensions.

unsurfaced_entries.name: Lee (2023) — Learning Sciences & Learning Engineering: Natural or Artificial Distinction?
unsurfaced_entries.description: Scholarly article examining the relationship between learning sciences and learning engineering. Argues for integration rather than separation, tracing epistemological differences and convergences.

unsurfaced_entries.name: Kolodner (2023) — Learning Engineering: What It Is, Why I'm Involved
unsurfaced_entries.description: Personal essay and scholarly reflection by Janet Kolodner on the nature of learning engineering, its roots in learning sciences, and her reasons for engagement with the field.

unsurfaced_entries.name: MIT LEAP Group — Learning Engineering and Practice
unsurfaced_entries.description: MIT research group applying learning engineering principles to university-level STEM education. Produces open courseware, edX materials, and research on evidence-based pedagogy at scale.

unsurfaced_entries.name: AECT Learning Engagement Activated Podcast — Jim Goodell Episode (2022)
unsurfaced_entries.description: Podcast episode featuring Jim Goodell discussing learning engineering principles, the ICICLE community, and the future of evidence-based learning design in professional development contexts.

unsurfaced_entries.name: SchoolSims — LE-Based Teacher Preparation Simulator (2025)
unsurfaced_entries.description: Simulation platform for teacher preparation applying learning engineering principles, cognitive task analysis, and naturalistic decision-making. Uses scenario-based assessment and data-driven iteration.

unsurfaced_entries.name: Learning Engineering at a Glance (Schatz, Goodell & Kessler, Army Press, 2023)
unsurfaced_entries.description: Concise overview of learning engineering for military and defense training contexts, published by US Army Press. Bridges LE field concepts with high-consequence domain workforce applications.

unsurfaced_entries.name: State of XR & Immersive Learning Report 2021 (iLRN / ICICLE)
unsurfaced_entries.affiliation_or_venue: Immersive Learning Research Network / IEEE ICICLE
unsurfaced_entries.description: Annual state-of-the-field report on extended reality (XR/VR/AR) in learning contexts, co-produced with ICICLE. Covers research landscape, practitioner adoption, and evidence base for immersive learning.

unsurfaced_entries.name: Data Use Throughout the Learning Engineering Process (ICICLE graphic)
unsurfaced_entries.description: Infographic and supporting documentation mapping how different types of data — formative, summative, behavioral, survey — are collected and used at each stage of the LE process.

unsurfaced_entries.name: Learning Engineering Adjacent Academic Programs Directory (ICICLE)
unsurfaced_entries.description: Google Doc directory of academic programs adjacent to or incorporating learning engineering, including LE-specific degrees, learning sciences programs, educational technology programs, and HCI programs.

unsurfaced_entries.name: Learning Engineering is a Team Sport (ICICLE presentation, 2024)
unsurfaced_entries.description: ICICLE presentation emphasizing the collaborative, multidisciplinary nature of learning engineering teams. Addresses how different domain experts contribute to LE projects and how to build effective teams.

unsurfaced_entries.name: Teaming Up to Improve Medical & Healthcare Education (Kurzweil & Marcellas, 2020)
unsurfaced_entries.description: Case study and practitioner article applying learning engineering team approaches to medical and healthcare professional education. Demonstrates LE in a high-stakes, complex domain.

unsurfaced_entries.name: Build a Learning Data Dream Team (Torrance, Lin & Goodell, ATD, 2024)
unsurfaced_entries.description: Practitioner article on assembling multidisciplinary teams to leverage learning data effectively. Covers roles (data analyst, learning engineer, instructional designer) and collaborative workflows.

unsurfaced_entries.name: IDEO Design Thinking Process (ideou.com)
unsurfaced_entries.description: IDEO's publicly available overview of the human-centered design thinking process. Referenced in LE as a precursor and complement to the iterative LE process, particularly for empathy and problem framing stages.

unsurfaced_entries.name: Van Campenhout et al. (2023) — Student-Centered Educational Data Science Through Learning Engineering
unsurfaced_entries.description: Research article integrating educational data science methods within a learning engineering framework. Demonstrates how student-centered analysis and iterative design reinforce each other in practice.

unsurfaced_entries.name: IEEE 1484.1-2003: Learning Technology Systems Architecture (LTSA)
unsurfaced_entries.description: Defines the high-level system components and communication protocols required for interoperable learning technology platforms. Foundational systems architecture standard for the field.
unsurfaced_entries.notes: IEEE LTSC sponsoring body. Architectural foundation underpinning xAPI, AIS, and LOM interoperability.

unsurfaced_entries.name: IEEE 9274 Family (xAPI / Experience API)
unsurfaced_entries.description: Data instrumentation standard governing how learning experiences are tracked, stored in Learning Record Stores (LRS), and analyzed across distributed platforms. Includes JSON-LD application profiles, cybersecurity protocols, and vocabulary registries.
unsurfaced_entries.notes: The data infrastructure standard underpinning modern LE telemetry pipelines. Supersedes SCORM for granular activity tracking.

unsurfaced_entries.name: IEEE P2247.1 & P2247.2: Adaptive Instructional Systems (AIS)
unsurfaced_entries.description: Classifies components, parameters, and interoperability protocols for adaptive instructional systems — engineering blueprints for building systems that dynamically adjust instruction based on real-time learner state.
unsurfaced_entries.notes: Most directly LE-relevant IEEE standard family. Defines the AIS reference model used in ICICLE technical guidance.

unsurfaced_entries.name: IEEE 1484.20.2: Recommended Practice for Defining Competencies
unsurfaced_entries.description: Rigorous data schema for defining human competencies. Supports Integrated Learner Records (ILR) spanning K-12, higher education, workforce, and military environments.
unsurfaced_entries.notes: Critical for competency-based education and credential portability. Underpins the field's move toward skills-based hiring.

unsurfaced_entries.name: IEEE 1484.12.1-2002 & P2881: Learning Object Metadata (LOM)
unsurfaced_entries.description: Essential standards for cataloging, discovery, and semantic structuring of digital learning resources across repositories. P2881 is the modernized successor incorporating linked data and accessibility requirements.
unsurfaced_entries.notes: Enables automated discoverability and cross-platform content reuse. P2881 extends LOM for modern web architectures.

unsurfaced_entries.name: Open Context Exchange (OCX)
unsurfaced_entries.description: OER schema: specification and metadata ontology for publishing Open Educational Resources on the web. Unifies content to a standard vocabulary for discoverability and automated remixing.
unsurfaced_entries.notes: Developed by Learning Tapestry / ICICLE. Enables interoperable OER ecosystems.

unsurfaced_entries.name: IEEE 1484.12.1-2002 & P2881: Learning Object Metadata
unsurfaced_entries.description: Essential standards for cataloging, discovery, and semantic structuring of digital learning resources across repositories. Enables automated discoverability and cross-platform content reuse.
unsurfaced_entries.notes: Landscape merge. Original ID: LE-LS-SG-005. Sponsoring body: IEEE LTSC. P2881 is the modernized successor incorporating linked data and accessibility requirements.

ieee_known_unsurfaced_unique.description: OER schema: specification and metadata ontology for publishing Open Educational Resources on the web. Unifies content to a standard vocabulary for discoverability and automated remixing.

ieee_known_unsurfaced_unique.title: IEEE 1484.12.1-2002 & P2881: Learning Object Metadata
ieee_known_unsurfaced_unique.description: Essential standards for cataloging, discovery, and semantic structuring of digital learning resources across repositories. Enables automated discoverability and cross-platform content reuse.

ieee_known_unsurfaced_unique.title: Learning Engineering (IEEE Press textbook)
ieee_known_unsurfaced_unique.description: The canonical textbook of the field. Two-part structure. End notes are a primary seed source for citation expansion. IEEE Press, 2022.

ieee_known_unsurfaced_unique.title: IEEE ICICLE Resources Page
ieee_known_unsurfaced_unique.description: Curated resource list maintained by IEEE ICICLE community. Includes papers, tools, programs, and community resources. Lacks cohesion and learning journey structure — this corpus addresses that gap.

ieee_known_unsurfaced_unique.title: Learning Engineering Adoption Maturity Model (LEAMM)
ieee_known_unsurfaced_unique.description: Organizational capability matrix (2024/2025) allowing enterprises to assess and advance their LE practice across human-centered design, learning science integration, and data-driven iteration dimensions. Provides a structured pathway from ad-hoc instructional design toward mature learning engineering practice. The field's primary organizational self-assessment tool, developed by the ICICLE community including Goodell, Craig, Blake-Plock, and colleagues.

ieee_known_unsurfaced_unique.title: IEEE Transactions on Learning Technologies
ieee_known_unsurfaced_unique.description: Covers technical design and architecture of learning systems: adaptive algorithms, NLP conversational agents, multimodal telemetry (eye tracking, EEG, galvanic skin response), and large-scale platform engineering. Requires substantive technical innovation in computer science alongside pedagogical validity. Key venue for AI-in-education engineering papers and Transformer-based conversational agent research.

ieee_known_unsurfaced_unique.title: Journal of Learning Engineering
ieee_known_unsurfaced_unique.description: The only journal specifically dedicated to learning engineering as a named field. Diamond Open Access — no fees for authors or readers. Community-driven by ICICLE; provides the 'Learning Engineering Primer' to align complex multidisciplinary terminology among authors. Because the field is young and sparsely indexed, JoLE papers often do not appear in citation-network-based discovery — venue search is required. Added as a venue query in icicle_adjacent_conference_queries.json.

ieee_known_unsurfaced_unique.title: IEEE ICICLE — Industry Consortium on Learning Engineering
ieee_known_unsurfaced_unique.description: Professional standards body for learning engineering. Produces the LE Adoption Maturity Model, competency frameworks, annual conference proceedings, and the Journal of Learning Engineering. Chartered 2017 under IEEE LTSC.

placeholder_url_proposals.proposed_description: Scholarly analysis of the relationship between learning sciences and learning engineering, arguing for tighter integration of the two.

placeholder_url_proposals.registry_description: Personal essay and scholarly reflection by Janet Kolodner on the nature of learning engineering, its roots in learning sciences, and her reasons for engagement with the field.

placeholder_url_proposals.proposed_description: Reflective essay on the origins, purpose, and practical value of learning engineering from a leading field contributor.
